symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric bilateral wrist/MCP/PIP synovitis with prolonged morning stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric small-joint synovitis with >1h morning stiffness is the RA phenotype.
Bilateral symmetric involvement is not the pattern of monoarticular joint sepsis.
Spongy synovitis with MCP involvement and prolonged stiffness is against OA.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single hot/red joint, no tracking erythema, no high fever/rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a single fiery joint and high fever argues strongly against joint sepsis.
No tracking erythema argues against spreading soft-tissue infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Flare precipitated by stopping the usual NSAID

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear temporal link to medication cessation supports an inflammatory flare that will respond to resuming the NSAID.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to open medication/prepare food while living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of hand function while isolated risks poor nutrition/hydration and medication non-administration.
A strong prior NSAID response supports restarting it for this flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric spongy synovitis, warmth, no erythema/tracking, normal cap refill (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric boggy synovitis without erythema/tracking fits an inflammatory flare, not infection.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetric hand/wrist synovitis with prolonged stiffness, no single septic-looking joint, no tracking erythema, only low fever, and a clear trigger of stopping the usual NSAID, is an inflammatory flare that should respond to resuming the NSAID — with septic arthritis/cellulitis safety-netted and an in-person joint review plus practical support arranged.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm synovitis pattern and exclude a septic joint/spreading infection; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, CRP, ESR, RF/anti-CCP) and rheumatology referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To characterise inflammation and progress DMARD planning for seronegative RA.
Not indicated for symmetric non-septic synovitis unless a single joint becomes disproportionately inflamed.
